editorial_thesis: Today’s practical edge is operational design: route models by consequence, keep identity and knowledge portable, make agent review inspectable, and patch by exposure.

topic_groups.title: Production Model Operations
topic_groups.selection_rationale: A coverage gap after yesterday’s measurement dossier: fresh production-routing data and a reusable export endpoint make model selection measurable rather than anecdotal.

sources.title: Vercel AI Gateway Production Index — July 2026
sources.why_selected: Aggregated production routing data shows open-weight models at 29% of token volume but under 4% of spend.
sources.usefulness: Use its routing pattern to define Buildn tiers for bulk, review, and consequential work.

sources.title: Access and share AI Gateway leaderboard data
sources.why_selected: It turns production-adoption charts into downloadable, queryable data under CC BY 4.0.
sources.usefulness: Use the export endpoint for a lightweight monthly market-signal check.

topic_groups.title: Self-hosted Trust + Knowledge
topic_groups.selection_rationale: Identity and durable personal knowledge have not been selected recently; two material July releases make this a practical local-first lane instead of a generic tooling roundup.

sources.title: Pocket ID v2.11.0
sources.why_selected: The passkey-focused OIDC provider adds SSRF hardening and proxy CIDR controls shortly after becoming OIDC certified in v2.10.0.
sources.usefulness: A candidate identity boundary for smaller self-hosted Buildn/client utilities.

sources.title: Logseq 2.0.1 — DB version beta
sources.why_selected: The first DB-version 2.0 beta is a material architectural shift for a widely used local knowledge tool.
sources.usefulness: Test whether it can become a durable working-memory surface for project notes and research.

topic_groups.title: Agentic Code Review
topic_groups.selection_rationale: This deliberately rotates away from yesterday’s generic quality gates toward an inspectable open implementation and its operating constraints.

sources.title: Sashiko — agentic review of Linux kernel code changes
sources.why_selected: The Linux Foundation project exposes a concrete 11-stage review protocol rather than an opaque agent product.
sources.usefulness: Borrow its staged review design for high-risk Buildn/client pull requests.

sources.title: Self-Host Weekly — 17 July 2026
sources.why_selected: It records the maintainer-level signal around Sashiko: adoption is being debated as a review-assistance problem, not an autonomous commit problem.
sources.usefulness: Use it as context for how to position review agents with cautious maintainers.

topic_groups.title: Patch Priority + Exposure
topic_groups.selection_rationale: Security governance was last selected July 18; the July patch review carries concrete actively exploited identity and SharePoint findings, which justifies a material update.

sources.title: Zero Day Initiative — July 2026 Security Update Review
sources.why_selected: It provides an exposure-first ordering for a record-scale Microsoft release, including actively exploited AD FS and SharePoint flaws.
sources.usefulness: Use it to turn a vendor patch note into a short client/service exposure audit.

sources.title: Android Security Bulletin — July 2026
sources.why_selected: It adds an endpoint/mobile layer to an otherwise server-focused patch sweep.
sources.usefulness: Include managed Android devices in the same evidence-based patch posture.
